---
title: Storage
toc: true
---

<!-- ![Credit: <a href="https://wallpapers.com/wallpapers/storage-computer-hard-drives-5pguoy6qvy239ttm.html">Hard Drives in Abstract Computer Storage</a> by ken123 at <a href="https://wallpapers.com/">Wallpapers.com</a>](../media/data_storage.jpg){width=75% fig-align="left"} -->

![Credit: <a href="https://wallpapers.com/wallpapers/storage-computer-hard-drives-5pguoy6qvy239ttm.html">Hard Drives in Abstract Computer Storage</a> by ken123.](../media/data_storage.jpg){width=75% fig-align="left"}

## Overview

To support diverse data management and computational needs, the ND-HNC has partnered with the Center for Research Computing ([CRC](https://crc.nd.edu/)) to provide researchers with a shared, multi-tiered data storage infrastructure.

These resources are categorized into three distinct tiers based on the data's activity level and backup requirements:

| Storage Tier | Total Capacity | Mount Location | Primary Purpose | Backup Policy |
|--------------|----------------|----------------|-----------------| --------------|
| Active Storage | 50 TB	| /groups/hnc | Intermediate storage of publication-critical files. | Nightly |
| Cold Storage | 250 TB | /goldfs/hnc | Long-term storage srcdata, rawdata, publication files. | Nightly |
| Scratch | 500 TB | TBD | Temporary workspace for intermediate generation. | **None** |

<!-- Key Guidelines for Users:

- As storage is shared across the ND-HNC community, please:
    - Manage your allocations responsibly,
    - Migrate data to the appropriate tier as your project progresses (see Dataflow).
- Compute nodes do not have access to files stored in Tier 2.
- Tier 3 (Scratch) is intended only for transient data, do not store critical or irreplaceable files here. -->

## Access

- Any CRC user [account](https://docs.crc.nd.edu/new_user/obtain_account.html) that is part of the HNC group has full access to these resources.
- Contact [Nathan Muncy](people.qmd) to be added to the HNC group.

## Usage

Using storage tiers for their intended use is critical when multiple users are sharing common resources. Instead of dividing available resources across all ND-HNC affiliated labs, we currently allow __all users to have full access to all resources__. Such an approach:

- Ensures there is sufficient capacity to support large projects (e.g. HCP data).
- Supports multiple projects at various stages of completion.
- Requires active data management and coordination among the ND-HNC group.

As projects progress from data collection to publication, reference the graphic below for data migration and appropriate use-case:

```{dot}
//| fig-width: 9
//| fig-height: 6
digraph NDHNC_Data_Flow {
    // General graph settings
    fontname="Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif";
    node [fontname="Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif", shape=box, style="filled", width=2.2];
    edge [fontname="Helvetica,Arial,sans-serif", fontsize=12];
    rankdir=LR; // Left-to-right flow

    // Tier 2: Cold Storage (Blue Theme)
    subgraph cluster_tier2 {
        label = "Cold Storage\l(250 TB, Nightly Backups)\l";
        style = "filled";
        color = "#dbeafe";
        fillcolor = "#eff6ff";
        fontcolor = "#1e40af";

        tier2_raw [label="Original Data:\l- srcdata DICOM\l- rawdata NIfTI\l- Assessment metrics\l- Task responses\l", fillcolor="#3b82f6", fontcolor="white"];
        tier2_archive [label="Long-Term Archive:\l- Project code\l- Final dataframes\l- Statistical output\l- Figures\l", fillcolor="#1d4ed8", fontcolor="white"];
    }

    // Tier 3: Scratch Workspace (Red/Orange Theme - No Backups)
    subgraph cluster_tier3 {
        label = "Scratch Workspace\l(500 TB, NO BACKUPS)\l";
        style = "filled";
        color = "#fee2e2";
        fillcolor = "#fef2f2";
        fontcolor = "#991b1b";

        tier3_proc [label="Intermediate Data:\l- Preprocessing\l- Subject models\l- Group models\l", fillcolor="#ef4444", fontcolor="white"];
        tier3_trash [label="Purge / Delete\l", shape=box, fillcolor="#b91c1c", fontcolor="white"];
    }

    // Local store
    subgraph cluster_local {
        label = "Local Storage\l";
        style = "filled";
        color = "gray";
        fillcolor = "lightgray";
        fontcolor = "black";

        local_store [label="Complicated Data:\l- Difficult generation\l- Long runtime\l", fillcolor="darkgray", fontcolor="black"];
    }

    // Tier 1: Active Storage (Green Theme)
    subgraph cluster_tier1 {
        label = "Active Storage\l(50 TB, Nightly Backups)\l";
        style = "filled";
        color = "#dcfce7";
        fillcolor = "#f0fdf4";
        fontcolor = "#166534";

        tier1_active [label="Final Data:\l- Statistical Maps\l- Figures\l", fillcolor="#22c55e", fontcolor="white"];
    }

    // Data Flow Connections (Edges)

    // 1. Initial transfer for processing
    tier2_raw -> tier3_proc [label=" Transfer for\l analysis\l", color="#2563eb", fontcolor="#2563eb", weight=2];

    // 2. Moving important results to Tier 1
    tier3_proc -> tier1_active [label=" Results\l (pre-publication)\l", color="#16a34a", fontcolor="#16a34a", weight=2];

    // 3. Cleanup of intermediates
    tier3_proc -> tier3_trash [label=" Removed when\l unused\l", color="#dc2626", fontcolor="#dc2626", style="dotted"];
    tier3_proc -> local_store [label=" Manage large\l data footprints\l", color="black", fontcolor="black", dir="both"];

    // 4. Post-publication archiving
    tier1_active -> tier2_archive [label=" Paper accepted:\l Archive Tier 1 data\l", color="black", fontcolor="black"];
}
```

1. Cold Storage provides a secure location for organized (e.g. [BIDS](https://bids-specification.readthedocs.io/en/stable/)) original data.
1. Data is transferred to Scratch for preprocessing and modelling.
    - Unused intermediates are purged.
    - Long-term storage of complicated intermediates are managed locally.
1. Final results are transferred to Active Storage for manuscript generation.
1. Upon publication, files in Active Storage are transferred to Cold Storage.

Recommendations:

- Build the dataflow into your workflow/codebase.
- Maintain only the requisite intermediates for the next pipeline step.

## Policies

1. Storage of Personal Health Information (PHI) data is __NOT__ permitted.
1. Users or groups using a disproportionate amount of resources will be required to reduce their footprint or  ___risk losing access___.
1. Intermediate Data can be regenerated from Original Data and should not be stored in Cold or Active Storage.
1. Data stored in Scratch should be for active analyses only.
1. Storing third-party data (e.g. HCP, NKI, ABCD) is not permitted on Cold or Active Storage.
    - Only the Final Data should be maintained.


<!-- ## Details

### Tier 1

### Tier 2

### Tier 3 -->
